bins.name: ESRs require distinct regulatory analysis
bins.desc: GridStor advocates that ESR charging must be studied separately and distinctly from large customer loads in interconnection processes, as ESRs function as both supply and demand through time-shifting and are fundamentally different in operation and control characteristics.
quotes: In transmission system operations, ESR charging may look similar to a large customer load, but key differences distinguish ESR charging from large customer loads: specifically in market operations, ESR charging a time-shifting of supply, and a key reason that load serving entities have procured ESRs for resource adequacy.
To the extent that the Commission seeks to put new requirements on large loads, it is imperative that ESRs be considered distinctly and separately from large loads.
we ask that any Commission consideration of such coordinated study include the load side of paired facilities like ESRs, not only the generation side.

Therefore, we recommend that the Commission consider whether study of ESR charging should be more optimally conducted during the generator interconnection process and prior to a generator interconnection agreement.

bins.name: Flexible ESRs enable faster interconnection
bins.desc: GridStor emphasizes that flexible, curtailable ESRs accelerate large load interconnection by providing grid services and managing power swings on millisecond timescales, making them critical to solving data center reliability needs.
quotes: Energy Storage Resources (ESRs) are critical to helping get large loads online faster and reliably.
Battery storage is uniquely suited to solving this problem for data centers, providing real and reactive power services at hundreds of MW size on millisecond timescales.

the ANOPR's proposal to expedite interconnection for large loads that are curtailable and dispatchable could be extended to the studies of ESR charging where RTOs/ISOs or Transmission Owners study ESR charging as a load.
ESRs are not only fundamentally flexible and curtailable resources, but also they are premised on responding to power system needs.

bins.name: Coordinated ESR-load studies reduce upgrades
bins.desc: GridStor supports coordinated interconnection studies of paired ESRs and large loads at the same point of interconnection, reducing transmission network upgrades needed when they are studied independently.
quotes: Siting a large load near or at the same point of interconnection as a new generating facility could reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect only the load or only the generating facility.
we ask that any Commission consideration of such coordinated study include the load side of paired facilities like ESRs, not only the generation side.
ESR charging can be planned for when a paired large load is reduced, and ESR discharging can be planned for when a paired large load is increased.

bins.name: Co-location strategy reduces costs
bins.desc: Coordinated siting of large loads and ESRs at the same interconnection point lowers network upgrade costs for both resources.
quotes: Siting a large load near or at the same point of interconnection as a new generating facility could reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect only the load or only the generating facility.

bins.name: Integrated ESR-load control improves efficiency
bins.desc: Planning ESR charging when paired large loads decrease and discharging when loads increase creates integrated operations that improve overall system efficiency.
quotes: ESR charging can be planned for when a paired large load is reduced, and ESR discharging can be planned for when a paired large load is increased.

bins.name: Expedited study for flexible ESRs
bins.desc: GridStor supports fast-tracking transmission service studies for flexible and curtailable ESRs, citing 60 to 90 day precedents in existing RTO/ISO tariffs and requesting similar timelines for ESR charging studies.
quotes: the ANOPR's proposal to expedite interconnection for large loads that are curtailable and dispatchable could be extended to the studies of ESR charging where RTOs/ISOs or Transmission Owners study ESR charging as a load.
Precedent exists in RTO/ISO tariffs for load interconnection processes that need no longer than 60-90 days to execute fully, and similar timeframes could be applied to ESR charging studies.

Therefore, we recommend that the Commission consider whether study of ESR charging should be more optimally conducted during the generator interconnection process and prior to a generator interconnection agreement.

bins.name: ESRs should not face large load queue rules
bins.desc: GridStor opposes applying large load queuing and deposit requirements to ESRs, noting that ESRs already participate in generator interconnection processes with established deposit and readiness requirements.
quotes: it would be inappropriate and onerously duplicative to apply large loads queuing requirements as discussed in the ANOPR to apply to ESRs.

bins.name: Data center loads threaten grid stability
bins.desc: Data centers present large power demands with sharp consumption swings during model training runs that can destabilize the power system.
quotes: Because data centers have very large power demands, the sudden swings in their power consumption that occur during model training runs can destabilize the power system.

bins.name: ESRs function as dual supply-demand resources
bins.desc: ESRs differ from conventional supply by acting as both withdrawals from and injections into the power system, a fundamental characteristic reflected in Order 841.
quotes: ESRs are unlike conventional supply, in that they make withdrawals from the power system in addition to injections.

bins.name: ESRs dominate the interconnection queue
bins.desc: Standalone ESRs comprise nearly 900 GW of active interconnection requests, representing roughly 45 percent of the total interconnection queue by capacity.
quotes: Standalone ESRs today comprise nearly 900 GW of interconnection requests, roughly 45% of the volume by capacity of all current active generator interconnection requests.

bins.name: PJM integrated ESR study model
bins.desc: PJM already uses integrated processes to study ESR charging and discharging together, providing a model the Commission could adopt more broadly.
quotes: PJM and CAISO already study ESR charging and discharging in an integrated processes and could offer valuable insights to the Commission.

bins.name: CAISO integrated ESR study model
bins.desc: CAISO already uses integrated processes to study ESR charging and discharging together, providing a model the Commission could adopt more broadly.
quotes: PJM and CAISO already study ESR charging and discharging in an integrated processes and could offer valuable insights to the Commission.
